prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "前后摆腿" (leg swing (front and back)) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the front phase of a forward leg swing (dynamic stretch / activation): right leg supporting with foot flat on the ground; left leg swung forward and up to hip height with knee almost fully extended; torso upright, right hand placed on a wall or support to the right for balance; this is a controlled dynamic mobility movement; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip flexors (psoas) of the swinging leg, with eccentric load on the hamstring group. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus of the supporting leg and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors of the swinging leg; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group (eccentric load); "臀大肌 / 核心" pointing to the glute and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
